_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to pick out a character or an event in a story we know.

_source.w: We are learning to pick out a character or an event in a story we know.
y: Identifying characters and events shows we are following the story.
This helps us remember and talk about stories after we hear them.
Recognising story parts is the foundation for understanding more complex stories later.
eg: We match a toy or picture of a character to the right page in the book.
We point to the part of the story where an important event happened.
We select the character's image from a group when asked 'Who was in the story?'
We pick up a prop that represents a character from a familiar story.
We use a symbol to show an event from the story, like 'the bear went to sleep'.
We sequence two pictures to show what happened first and what happened next.
